---
title: "quarto-revealjs-freestyle "
subtitle: "<hr> *An advanced fork of quarto-revealjs-editable* : <br> Drag, resize and reposition (almost) anything on your slides !"
author: "Eric Bavu"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    center: true
    code-copy: false
revealjs-plugins:
  - editable
filters:
  - editable
execute:
  echo: false
---

# Plain text div

::: {.absolute width=310.749px height=369.537px left=1169.88px top=-111.852px}
<ul>

<li>Drag us anywhere on the slide.</li>

<li>Resize us with the handles.</li>

<li>Use **A+** / **A-** to change font size,</li>

<li>and **✎** to edit the text directly.</li>

</ul>
:::

# Markdown content


Citation blocks : 

::: {.absolute width=1536px height=NaNpx left=245.291px top=337.876px}
<blockquote>

“The best way to predict the future is to invent it.”

— Alan Kay

</blockquote>
:::

# Callout blocks


::: {.callout-tip}
# Tip
Freestyle does not handle very well with Quarto callout blocks at the moment when saving your modifications. Avoid using *freestyled* callouts or make a PR if you find a solution ! 
:::


# LaTeX equations


Fixed position equation : $TF[s(f)] = \hat{S}(f)$ 


::: {.absolute width=1536px height=NaNpx left=-16.8337px top=256.112px}

Movable : 
$$
\hat{S}(f) = \int_{-\infty}^{+\infty} s(t)\, e^{-2\pi i f t}\, dt
$$
:::


# Image

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="30%"}
::: {.callout-note}
## Tip
Hold **Shift** while resizing to preserve the aspect ratio ! 

But since it's **freestyle**, you can do whatever you want 
:::
:::
::: {.column width="70%"}
![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/f/ff/AresV_Ascent.jpg){.absolute width=329.741px height=185.373px left=88.9479px top=472.685px}
:::
::::


# Video embed

::: {.absolute width=793.14px height=463.571px left=1223.08px top=-273.956px}
{{< video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=U5qgzAmGwtI width="800" height="450" >}}
:::


# It even works with executable code

>
> ### Yum, resizable plotly interactive plots !
> - not shown here since I can't be sure you have `Plotly` or `matplotlib` installed, but if you have, juste add braces around `python` in the following code-cell ! 

::: {.absolute width=1536px height=NaNpx left=257.315px top=100.96px}

```python
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
# see https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dupin_cyclide
import numpy as np
a, b, d = 1.32, 1., 0.8
c = a**2 - b**2
u, v = np.mgrid[0:2*np.pi:100j, 0:2*np.pi:100j]
x = (d * (c - a * np.cos(u) * np.cos(v)) + b**2 * np.cos(u)) / (a - c * np.cos(u) * np.cos(v))
y = b * np.sin(u) * (a - d*np.cos(v)) / (a - c * np.cos(u) * np.cos(v))
z = b * np.sin(v) * (c*np.cos(u) - d) / (a - c * np.cos(u) * np.cos(v))

fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Surface(x=x, y=y, z=z, surfacecolor=x**2 + y**2 + z**2))
fig.update_layout(title_text="Ring cyclide", plot_bgcolor='rgba(0,0,0,0)', paper_bgcolor='rgba(0,0,0,0)', height=400)
fig.show(config={
    'displaylogo': False,
    'modeBarButtonsToRemove': ['toImage', 'zoomIn', 'zoomOut']
    })
``` 
:::

# Freestyle is in the air 


::: {.callout-warning}
## Did you notice the floating bar at bottom ? 

- It is hidden if you did not move anything or if you already exported your modifications
- Whenever you want, just click on **save edits** or **copy to clipboard**
- The saved qmd holds the absolute positions, sizes, attributes of your elements !
- Even better, just tap copy to clipboard, paste it in your text editor on top of the current qmd, and validate your freestyle modifications ! 
:::

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="20%"}

:::

::: {.column width="60%"}

![](https://img.ccnull.de/1100000/preview/1101829_8649dc8b97dc7018480a909e775407ae.jpg)

:::


::: {.column width="20%"}

:::
::::

# Enjoy !
